rationale: Author-of-record lens: the diff delivers plan §Bead 2 Steps 1-5 and all ACs exactly. Step 1 (R5) reproduces plan.go:142-146 byte-identically — loadImpactedDomains(specDir), normalizeImpactedDomains(nil, root, "", impacted), impacted-domains-resolve per err, plus the same impacted-domains-load guard (errors.Unwrap + os.IsNotExist). Step 2 (R6) uses the exact widened regex `\[(ADR-\d{4})[^\]]*\]\([^)]+\)`, sections["ADR Touchpoints"], and newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(root, specDir)) identical to plan.go:156; existence-only, emits adr-touchpoint-missing with a recovery hint. Steps 3/4 add the two named tests with exactly the specified 3 and 5 subtests, scanning r.Issues for code+SevError (not FormatText). Step 5 doc-sync region lands in architecture.md with the R7c boundary. Wiring sits after the required-section checks, before checkScopeSubsections. Verified: both named tests PASS, ./internal/validate and ./internal/approve green, go build ./... clean, branch-built binary passes 110's own spec (exit 0), doc file in HEAD, git status clean. internal/approve untouched (3-file commit).
findings: R5/R6 parity confirmed byte-identical to plan.go: same normalizeImpactedDomains call + impacted-domains-resolve code + impacted-domains-load guard (Step 1), same newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(...)) and widened regex (Step 2).

Non-blocking, parity-neutral: checkADRTouchpointsExist adds a `seen` dedup map not named in the plan's Step 2 text — it only collapses repeated identical missing IDs to one error and cannot change any pass/fail outcome; a reasonable improvement, not a deviation.
R6 returns early when the ADR Touchpoints section is absent ('already flagged by the required-section check') — correct, avoids double-reporting; matches plan intent.

All 8 Verification checklist items pass on the pinned SHA (both named tests, validate+approve packages, branch-built self-check on 110's own spec exit 0, doc-sync file in HEAD, go build ./...); working tree matches the commit and git status is clean.
